flags.concern: Reaction moderation: the plan only marks bot-initiated removals in ReactorCog's restricted-emoji branch, but the repo already has other bot `message.remove_reaction(...)` paths. Those removals will still flow through `on_raw_reaction_remove` without a marker and be misclassified as `user_self_removal`.
flags.evidence: Addressed three significant flags: (1) Added a shared `register_bot_removal` marker mechanism on ReactorCog and inserted 4 call sites in `src/features/curating/curator.py` so curator `❌` cleanups are classified as `bot_curator_reject` rather than misclassified as self-removals. (2) Renamed `bot_clear_all`/`bot_clear_emoji` to `moderator_cleared_all`/`moderator_cleared_emoji` — the clear events lack actor info but the repo has no bot clear calls, so mod attribution is accurate; added enumeration of active reactors via a new `get_active_reactors` DB helper so each cleared reaction produces its own row. (3) Added a `message_deleted_cascade` classification and a new `on_raw_message_delete` listener on ReactorCog that enumerates active reactors and writes one row each, using a new `get_message_snapshot` DB helper to fetch author/content (preserved by LoggerCog's soft-delete). Updated CHECK constraint, expanded test scenarios, and clarified that the marker-consumer (on_raw_reaction_remove) is the sole writer for curator path (the restricted-emoji branch still writes eagerly because it has live objects in hand).

flags.concern: Reaction moderation: `bot_clear_all` / `bot_clear_emoji` are not supportable from the clear-event payloads. The event does not identify who cleared the reactions, so those classifications would over-claim bot attribution and be wrong for moderator/manual clears.
flags.evidence: Addressed three significant flags: (1) Added a shared `register_bot_removal` marker mechanism on ReactorCog and inserted 4 call sites in `src/features/curating/curator.py` so curator `❌` cleanups are classified as `bot_curator_reject` rather than misclassified as self-removals. (2) Renamed `bot_clear_all`/`bot_clear_emoji` to `moderator_cleared_all`/`moderator_cleared_emoji` — the clear events lack actor info but the repo has no bot clear calls, so mod attribution is accurate; added enumeration of active reactors via a new `get_active_reactors` DB helper so each cleared reaction produces its own row. (3) Added a `message_deleted_cascade` classification and a new `on_raw_message_delete` listener on ReactorCog that enumerates active reactors and writes one row each, using a new `get_message_snapshot` DB helper to fetch author/content (preserved by LoggerCog's soft-delete). Updated CHECK constraint, expanded test scenarios, and clarified that the marker-consumer (on_raw_reaction_remove) is the sole writer for curator path (the restricted-emoji branch still writes eagerly because it has live objects in hand).

flags.concern: Reaction moderation: message-delete cascades are actually distinguishable in this codebase, so dropping them leaves a real coverage gap against the brief.
